title: Lesson 39 – Reasons & Despite (ので / のに)
description: Gründe und Gegensätze ausdrücken: V-te/N-de (Grund für unwillkürliches Ergebnis), ので (objektiver Grund), のに (obwohl/trotzdem), 途中で (unterwegs/mittendrin).

vocabulary.meaning: to be late, to be delayed
on the way, in the middle of

grammar.title: V-て / N-で (Reason for Involuntary Result)
grammar.structure: V-て form / N で、〜 (involuntary result)
grammar.explanation: The te-form of a verb or で after a noun can express a cause or reason, especially when the result is something involuntary or beyond the speaker's control. The result is typically an emotional reaction, a state, or an uncontrollable consequence. This cannot be used when the result is a deliberate action.
grammar.example_sentences:
① 事故が あって、電車が 遅れました。
  (Jiko ga atte, densha ga okuremashita.)
  — There was an accident, and the train was delayed.
② 地震で ビルが 壊れました。
  (Jishin de biru ga kowaremashita.)
  — The building was destroyed by the earthquake.

grammar.title: ～ので (Because — Objective Reason)
grammar.explanation: ので connects two sentences where the first is the reason for the second. It presents the reason objectively and sounds softer and more polite than から. Before ので, na-adjectives and nouns use な instead of だ (e.g. 静かなので, 学生なので). ので is preferred in formal situations and when giving reasons to superiors.
grammar.example_sentences:
③ 電車が 遅れたので、会社に 遅刻しました。
  (Densha ga okureta node, kaisha ni chikoku shimashita.)
  — Because the train was delayed, I was late for work.
④ このレストランは おいしいので、いつも 込んでいます。
  (Kono resutoran wa oishii node, itsumo konde imasu.)
  — Because this restaurant is delicious, it's always crowded.

grammar.title: ～のに (Although / Despite)
grammar.explanation: のに connects two sentences where the second is an unexpected or contradictory result of the first. It expresses the speaker's surprise, dissatisfaction, or regret. Before のに, na-adjectives and nouns use な instead of だ. のに often carries a nuance of complaint or disappointment.
grammar.example_sentences:
⑤ 一生懸命 勉強したのに、試験に 落ちました。
  (Isshoukenmei benkyou shita noni, shiken ni ochimashita.)
  — Although I studied hard, I failed the exam.
⑥ 約束したのに、来ませんでした。
  (Yakusoku shita noni, kimasen deshita.)
  — Although we made a promise, they didn't come.

grammar.title: 途中で (In the Middle of / On the Way)
grammar.structure: V-dict + 途中で / N の 途中で
grammar.explanation: 途中で means 'in the middle of' or 'on the way'. It indicates that something happens while another action is in progress or while going somewhere. With verbs, the dictionary form is used before 途中で. With nouns (often places), の途中で is used.
grammar.example_sentences:
⑦ 会社へ 行く 途中で、友達に 会いました。
  (Kaisha e iku tochuu de, tomodachi ni aimashita.)
  — On the way to the office, I met a friend.
⑧ 映画を 見ている 途中で、寝て しまいました。
  (Eiga o mite iru tochuu de, nete shimaimashita.)
  — I fell asleep in the middle of watching a movie.

conversation.title: I was late because of the traffic jam
conversation.title_ja: 渋滞で 遅れたんです

lines.speaker: Sato
lines.japanese: すみません、遅く なりました。

lines.speaker: Sato
lines.japanese: 事故が あって、道が 渋滞していたので、遅れたんです。
lines.romaji: Jiko ga atte, michi ga juutai shite ita node, okureta n desu.
lines.english: There was an accident and the road was jammed, so I was late.

lines.speaker: Tanaka
lines.japanese: そうですか。大変でしたね。
lines.romaji: Sou desu ka. Taihen deshita ne.

lines.speaker: Sato
lines.japanese: せっかく 早く 出たのに、結局 いつもより 遅く なって しまいました。
lines.romaji: Sekkaku hayaku deta noni, kekkyoku itsumo yori osoku natte shimaimashita.
lines.english: Although I specially left early, I ended up being later than usual.

lines.speaker: Tanaka
lines.japanese: 途中で 電話できませんでしたか。
lines.romaji: Tochuu de denwa dekimasen deshita ka.
lines.english: Couldn't you call on the way?

lines.speaker: Sato
lines.japanese: すみません。携帯の 電池が なかったので、電話できなかったんです。
lines.romaji: Sumimasen. Keitai no denchi ga nakatta node, denwa dekinakatta n desu.
lines.english: I'm sorry. My phone battery was dead, so I couldn't call.

lines.speaker: Tanaka
lines.japanese: そうですか。まあ、無事で よかったです。
lines.romaji: Sou desu ka. Maa, buji de yokatta desu.
lines.english: I see. Well, I'm glad you're safe.
